channel.title: Feedback on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Feedback on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Toxic Positivity
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about toxic positivity and I really want to talk about this.

item.title: Handling Advice
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today, I want to talk about handling advice.

item.title: Collecting Feedback
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about collecting feedback, specifically how and when and what to do with the feedback that you get on the game you’re making.

item.title: More Thoughts On Game Marketing
item.description: Hi everybody. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to give more thoughts on game marketing and look, I exist in daytime.

item.title: Do Devs Know What Gamers Want?
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about do devs know what Gamers want.

item.title: Do Gamers Know What They Like?
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about the question: do game players even know what they like?

item.title: Feedback Needs Context
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how feedback needs context, because it’s hard to know what to do with feedback if you don’t have context.

item.title: My Memoirs, Part 2
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about my Memoirs again, so consider this a my Memoirs video, part two.

item.title: Editing Your Game Story
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about Axion as a hypothetical particle to explain dark matter through their ability to violate CP symmetry in Quantum chromodynamics — wait, wrong Channel.

item.title: "Git Gud"
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today let’s talk about the phrase get good.

item.title: How To Receive Criticism
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about how to receive criticism.

item.title: Giving Good Feedback
item.description: Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to answer another question, this time from Brooke Richardson, 1373, who asks: can you do a video on giving good and relevant game design feedback, and how do we know when things aren’t balanced?
